content.problemStatement:
Write a C program that takes an integer representing the day of the week (1 to 7) as input and prints the corresponding day's name. 

If the given integer is any number other than 1 to 7 - then you need to output 'Invalid day number'.

Use a switch statement to implement this.

Check the sample input / output below to for further clarity.
content.inputFormat: The input is a single integer representing the day of the week (1 to 7).
content.outputFormat: Print the corresponding day's name. If the given integer is any number other than 1 to 7, print 'Invalid day number'.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Input day is 2, so the output is Tuesday
Input day is 7, so the output is Sunday
Input day is 10, which is not between 1 and 7, so the output is Invalid day number

solution.approach: Use a switch statement to map the input day to its corresponding day name. If the input day is not between 1 and 7, print 'Invalid day number'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) because the switch statement has a constant number of cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) because the space used does not grow with the input size
